description: GenerateMotifDatabase can be used to obtain position weight matrix (PWM) data to score binding sites in other TFSites modules. This tool extracts the PFM(s) for one or more transcription factor(s) of interest and then converts each PFM to its respective PWM.
author: Joe Solvason
plan:
## tfsites.GenerateMotifDatabase GenePattern Module — Implementation Plan

### 1. Overview
tfsites.GenerateMotifDatabase is a GenePattern module that GenerateMotifDatabase can be used to obtain position weight matrix (PWM) data to score binding sites in other TFSites modules. This tool extracts the PFM(s) for one or more transcription factor(s) of interest and then converts each PFM to its respective PWM.

### 2. Parameters
The module exposes 6 parameters:
- Required: PFM.input.file, input.format, use.pseudocounts.to.calculate.score, output.filename
- Optional: filter.keywords, background.nucleotide.frequencies

### 3. Command Line
```
python /build/tfsites-webportal/02-generateMotifDatabase/02-generateMotifDatabase.py -i <PFM.input.file> -b <background.nucleotide.frequencies> -p <use.pseudocounts.to.calculate.score>  <filter.keywords> -o <output.filename>  -f <input.format>
```

### 4. Docker Image
Docker image: genepattern/tfsites\:16.3

### 5. Validation Strategy
- Validate all required parameters are provided
- Validate file formats match expected extensions
- Check parameter value ranges where applicable

### 6. Implementation Roadmap
Phase 1: Wrapper script implementation
Phase 2: Docker container setup
Phase 3: Parameter validation and error handling
Phase 4: Testing and documentation

parameters.name: PFM.input.file
parameters.description: File containing multiple PFMs. It is formatted similar to a fasta file where the header line starts with ">" and is followed by that PFM.

parameters.name: input.format
parameters.description: Input whether the motif database is a PFM (counts of each nt at each position) or PWM (frequency of each nt at each position weighted by the background frequency of the genomes and adjusted by pseudocounts).

parameters.name: filter.keywords
parameters.description: File containing a single column with a list of keywords. The keywords can be the transcription factor name and/or matrix ID.

parameters.name: use.pseudocounts.to.calculate.score
parameters.description: Choose if you want to use pseudocounts to calculate the PWM score for binding sites.

parameters.name: background.nucleotide.frequencies
parameters.description: Choose the nucleotide background frequencies in the order of A, T, G, and C.

parameters.name: output.filename
parameters.description: Base name of the output files.
